mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for serving large language models efficiently and reliably under high-scale traffic. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the llm-d CLI tool to manage deployments and configurations.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d version

```

> Download the CLI binary for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster by updating the kubeconfig file.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes to ensure they are in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
Deploy llm-d using Helm to manage the application lifecycle.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configurations.
> Apply monitoring configurations.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
Remove llm-d and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the llm-d release.
> Delete the cluster and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, upgrade the llm-d installation, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade llm-d to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes to meet resource requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service is not reachable, possibly due to incorrect service configuration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the correct ports are exposed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for advanced scheduling features and improved autoscaling capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
